name: Hypertechno
description: Emerged in the 2020s by mixing fast tempos, prominent basslines, and hard-hitting drums derived from popular Techno with simplistic, catchy melodies and vocals, often influenced by 2000s Hard Dance, Eurodance and Electropop.

name: Deathstep
description: Darker, heavier form of Brostep that uses "machine gun" basses, atonal synths, and elements from genres like Death Metal and Metalcore.

name: Space Ambient
description: Flowing and relaxing synthesizer-based music derived from Progressive Electronic, often oriented around imagery of outer space.

name: Broken Beat
description: Choppy, syncopated grooves and loose influence from 1970s Jazz-Funk, with eclectic roots spanning Drum and Bass, Deep House, Acid Jazz, and Afrobeat.

name: Electro Hop
description: Emerged in the mid 2000s featuring upbeat rap verses and catchy hooks over four-on-the-floor Electropop and Electro House-inspired production.

name: Bubblegum Bass
description: Originated in the early 2010s, exaggerating the cutest and most feminine parts of 2000s Pop and combining them with bouncy, plastic synths over rhythms and sounds derived from a variety of contemporary club styles.

name: Jersey Club
description: Emerged in Newark in the late 2000s; features fast tempos, hard kicks, and less prominent breakbeats.

name: Digital Cumbia
description: Mixes an Electronic sound into traditional Cumbia through influences from House, Dancehall, Hyphy, and Dubstep.

name: Acid House
description: Emerged in the mid-1980s, characterised by repetitive basslines created with the distinctive modulated "squelching" sound of the Roland TB-303 synthesiser.

name: Balearic Beat
description: Variety of relaxed, laid-back music that originates from the Balearic Islands off the coast of Spain, and particularly the club scene in Ibiza.

name: Comfy Synth
description: Calm, peaceful and soothing sounds, as well as nostalgic, sentimental, or childish atmospheres and aesthetics; often incorporates wholesome themes while utilizing lo-fi or hazy recordings and influences from New Age.

name: Glitch Pop
description: Incorporates elements of more experimental Electronic styles like IDM and Glitch.

name: Post-Dubstep
description: Minimalistic, atmospheric sound design and eclectic drum patterns, derived from but not fitting Dubstep.

name: Big Room House
description: Energetic, festival-oriented style characterized by heavy reverb, intense build-ups, strong kicks, minimalistic drop melodies, and sparse, raw breakdowns.

name: Moombahcore
description: High-pitched synth leads, gritty percussion, a kick-snare pattern, aggressive bass drops, and around 110 BPM while often paralleling Complextro or Brostep.

name: Psytrance
description: Emphasizes a psychedelic feel and an energetic sound; based around influences from Goa Trance and Acid Trance.

name: Neurofunk
description: Focuses on technical songwriting with warped, complex bass sounds, distorted and modulated synths (often with repeated stabs over the basslines), and dark but clean production.

name: Ghetto Funk
description: Rich sampling, drawing from classic Hip Hop as well as from Funk and Soul, and strong midtempo break grooves.

name: Downtempo
description: Atmospheric and groove-based with relaxed tempos and mellow beats.

name: Vaportrap
description: Incorporates a blend of Trap percussion set to the re-utilization of old samples, such as 1990s pop culture or old computer library sounds.

name: Funk mandelão
description: Simple lyricism, raw and minimalistic production, and distinct drops with bursting bass.

name: Bassline
description: Strong emphasis on intricate basslines as well as four-on-the-floor beat patterns.

name: 2-Step
description: Originating in London and popular in the late 1990s; jittery, shuffling rhythms rarely following a four-on-the-floor pattern.

name: Hard Techno
description: Typically rave-oriented form characterized by hard, prominent kick drums, pounding rhythms, fast tempos around 140-160 BPM, and aggressive, often distorted sounds.

name: Spacesynth
description: Highly melodic, often instrumental futuristic strain, formed under the heavy influence of Space Disco, Progressive Electronic and, later, early Eurobeat.

name: Futurepop
description: Derivative of EBM, retaining its apocalyptic worldview but incorporating heavy influence from the melodic style of Synthpop.

name: Singeli
description: Developed in Tanzania in the mid-2000s; fuses incredibly rapid beats, usually between 180-300BPM, with MCing and musical influences from older Tanzanian genres.

name: Trip Hop
description: Evokes a surreal, trippy, dreamy, and sometimes dark atmosphere with offbeat turntable scratches, light vocal melodies, and Hip Hop-influenced beats.

name: Ambient Dub
description: Takes cues from Ambient and Dub reggae, featuring the atmosphere of the former and the Jamaican-style basslines, percussion, and psychedelic production techniques of the latter.

name: Ghettotech
description: Fuses the syncopation and synthesized sounds of Electro and Detroit Techno with the cut-up samples of Ghetto House and fast rhythms of Miami Bass.

name: Chicago House
description: Umbrella term for a broad variety of House that originated from Chicago in the (mostly mid-to-late) 1980s and early 90s.

name: Full-On Psytrance
description: Faster and more intense, usually set around 145-150 BPM and featuring harder rolling basslines, vocal samples and "spacey" effects.

name: Hardtek
description: Modern version of the "tribecore" sound featuring deep alternating basses and festival EDM-influenced drops.

name: Hip House
description: Incorporates production elements of early Hip Hop and usually rapping into 1980s House.

name: Happy Hardcore
description: Emerged in the 1990s from the UK Breakbeat Hardcore scene; characterized by saccharine vocals, uplifting piano riffs and spacey effects, gradually developing to emphasize extremely fast-paced four-on-the-floor beats.

name: Dubstep
description: Half-time syncopated and sparse beats set around 140 BPM with heavy sub-basses; emerged in South London in the early 2000s.

name: Speedcore
description: Exceedingly fast tempos, frequently above 300 BPM, and often aggressive themes and samples.

name: Flex Dance Music
description: Largely instrumental; infused with Electronic Dance Music elements.

name: Bitpop
description: Fuses Bit Music with additional synths, beats, guitars and modern production values, emphasizing highly catchy melodies and relatively fast tempos.

name: Synthwave
description: Rooted in 1980s synth music, characterized by large, throbbing "retro" synths and visual elements, often features upbeat percussion and/or epic, cinematic atmospheres.

name: Liquid Riddim
description: Distinct for its atmospheric production, overpowering ambience, watery synths, and melodic bass patterns.

name: Melodic Dubstep
description: Melodious, tuneful drops, rich, lush synth leads, powerful stretched chords, and light and harmonious sound design.

name: Gospel House
description: Incorporates heavy Gospel elements and singing borrowed from classic and contemporary gospel, usually in the form of choir vocals and call-and-response chants.

name: Riddim
description: Repetitive and relatively simplistic style inspired by mid-to-late-2000s UK Dubstep, often using robotic and/or squishy sound design along with bouncy square basses and less prominent buildups.

name: Hi-NRG
description: Emerged in the late 1970s and quickly became the most popular form of Electronic Dance Music in the LGBT club scenes of San Francisco and New York, characterized by its uptempo, high-energy sound.

name: Minimal Drum and Bass
description: Minimalist, sparse sound; emerged in two main 2000s strains, one Dubstep-inspired and one IDM-inspired, which crossed paths in the 2010s.

name: Electro-Industrial
description: Formed in the late 1980s and early 1990s; grew out of a combination of EBM and Industrial.

name: Big Room Trance
description: Incorporates production features associated with Big Room House and dirty, distorted leads influenced by Electro House, Dutch House and Hardstyle.

name: Belgian Techno
description: Early form of Hardcore, resulting from the further evolution of New Beat due to the strong influence of Techno and EBM.

name: Breakstep
description: Hard-hitting Breakbeat rhythms and heavy, rolling basslines.

name: Moombahton
description: Offshoot of Dutch House characterized by tempos in the 100-110 BPM range, Reggaetón-influenced rhythms, bass-heavy kicks and high-pitched synths.

name: Psystyle
description: Prominent Psytrance influences, especially the basslines and energy of mainstream psytrance.

name: Hard Trap
description: Often abrasive subgenre of Trap with heavy influence from Hardstyle.

name: Space Disco
description: Emerged in Europe in the mid-to-late 1970s, characterized by layers of oscillating synth, space-oriented sound effects, and a futuristic sound inspired by contemporary sci-fi media.

name: Nu-Disco
description: 21st-century trend mixing modern, polished Electronic production with the aesthetics, structures, and lively, organic beats of 1970s and 1980s Disco.

name: Euro Trance
description: Emerged as a fusion with Eurodance, features fast tempos, more accessible grooves and structures, and simple, catchy melodies with vocals and saw synth leads.

name: Ballroom
description: Emerging from the predominantly Black and LGBT Harlem ballroom culture, it is characterized by hard-hitting rhythms, generally improvised chanting by MCs known as commentators, and a distinctive crash sample.

name: Phonk
description: Merges Funk automotivo's rhythms, vocals, and energy, with the energetic and nocturnal production typical of the Drift Phonk scene, and Phonk House.

name: Aggrotech
description: Merges aggressive-sounding synths and apocalyptic atmospheres with harsh 4/4 beats and fast tempos.

name: Afro House
description: Emerged by fusing Deep House with Southern African Music, characterized by hypnotizing drum patterns of shakers and congas, often sampled African vocals or soulful original features, and spiritual, organic, melodic atmosphere.

name: East Coast Club
description: Group of club styles from the US East Coast that combines Miami Bass rhythms with influence from Breakbeat and Ghetto House.

name: Hardcore Breaks
description: Revival of Breakbeat Hardcore which emerged in the 2000s, bringing in modern influences and production techniques.

name: Tech Trance
description: Late 1990s-emerging subgenre characterized by energetic percussion with loud kicks, filtered hi-hats and tribal drums; side-chained, detuned and repetitive leads, as well as other raw, distorted, and complex sounds influenced by Techno, Hard Trance and Electro House.

name: Future Riddim
description: Melodic and futuristic style inspired by Future Bass, and to a lesser extent Colour Bass and Melodic Dubstep.

name: UK Jackin'
description: Successor to British Bassline, characterized by strong basslines, dark atmospheres, and dominant grooves.

name: Gqom
description: Evolved from South African House, specifically Kwaito; often described as a more minimal and raw version.

name: Drumstep
description: Dancefloor-friendly, loud, and heavy combination of the drum programming and tempo of Jump-Up with the half-time rhythm and midrange LFO wobble of Brostep.

name: Lento violento
description: Features a "slow and violent" sound between 70-120 BPM with hard kicks inspired by Hardcore and Hardstyle.

name: Diva House
description: Dominant, anthemic female vocals, common use of piano stabs, and energetic, driving four-on-the-floor rhythms.

name: Peak Time Techno
description: Hard, dynamic, energetic and accessible subgenre featuring strong kicks, massive dirty synths, tempos around 130 BPM, more suspenseful structures with breaks and build-ups, and big, modern, festival-oriented sound; rose to high popularity in the late 2010s.

name: Progressive House
description: Trance-influenced strain of House that grew to prominence in the early 1990s, using polished production and gradual progressions.

name: Glitch
description: Uses abstract sound design and malfunctioning electronics—CD skips, data corruption, and hardware noise—as main compositional elements.

name: Future House
description: Features distinctive metallic-sounding drops and frequency-modulated basslines at around 120-130 BPM.

name: Folktronica
description: Combines sound design techniques from IDM and Glitch with acoustic instrumentation inspired by Contemporary Folk.

name: Dubwise Drum and Bass
description: Implementing Reggae and Dub influences, usually vocal dubs, delay effects, and reggae instrumentation, with the clean production and rhythms of post-2000s styles.

name: West Coast Breaks
description: Combines the earlier breakbeats of Hip Hop and early Breakbeat or even Miami Bass, with various 1990s Electronic Dance Music genres like Trance and Techno.

name: Future Bass
description: Woozy, detuned synthesizers, 7th chords, harsh basslines, syncopated percussion with fast-paced hi-hats and a melodic, carefree atmosphere.

name: Hardstyle
description: Hard, four-on-the-floor kick drums, fast tempo around 150 BPM, reverse bass and harsh, distorted synths.

name: Artcore
description: Combines an eclectic mix of Electronic Dance Music genres with the instrumentation and melodic stylings of genres like Cinematic Classical for a maximalist and emotional sound.

name: Electro Swing
description: Upbeat, energetic style that splices Swing with Electronic Dance Music, looking to recapture the atmosphere of late 1920s to mid-1940s Jazz within a more updated club-friendly medium.

name: Juke
description: Faster than Ghetto House, playing at 160bpm, and makes striking use of unconventional drum patterns which differ wildly from other House styles.

name: House
description: Generally features a four-on-the-floor beat within 110-140 BPM, a groove built on swing and syncopation, synthesized basslines, repetitive melodic elements, and an upbeat mood.

name: Weightless
description: Typically instrumental, leftfield Grime style characterized by sparse, "weightless" percussion and spectral, atmospheric production.

name: Frenchcore
description: Off-beat, bouncy rhythmic patterns and tempos around the 200 BPM range.

name: Amapiano
description: House from South Africa deriving from previous popular genres including Gqom, Kwaito, and Deep House.

name: Briddim
description: Combining the kick-clap rhythms and syncopation of Riddim with Brostep sound design.

name: Hyperpop
description: Developed and took inspiration from Bubblegum Bass and popular 2000s-2010s Electropop, characterized by eclectic, unconventional, mechanical-sounding and dense production; and by manipulated, usually auto-tuned vocals.

name: Berlin School
description: Atmospheric, multi-layered synthesizer textures; pioneered in Berlin in the 1970s as an offshoot to the Krautrock movement.

name: Uptempo Hardcore
description: Utilizes lo-fi production, harsh kicks, and elements borrowed from Rawstyle and other gritty Hardcore styles while usually sitting at around 185-220 BPM.

name: Synthpop
description: Led by melodic synthesizer sounds, often with reverberating drum machine patterns; commonly associated with the 1980s.

name: Gabber
description: Fast-paced and energetic, often in the 160-220 BPM range, with a hallmark of a distinctive distorted kick sound.

name: Italo Dance
description: Appeared in the 1990s after the decline of Italo-Disco, featuring rhythmic influence from that genre.

name: Deconstructed Club
description: Emerged in the 2010s as the hybridization of diverse Electronic Dance Music genres with an abrasive Post-Industrial sound.

name: Tearout
description: Emerged in the late 2000s, characterized by a gritty, aggressive sound and closely affiliated with early, deep Dubstep.

name: Purple Sound
description: Emerged in the late 2000s out of the Dubstep scene of the time, incorporating synth leads and basslines inspired by 1980s Synth Funk and 1990s G-Funk, and Video Game Music, into a rhythmic palette mostly derived from dubstep and Grime.

name: Rawphoric
description: Combines raw harsh kicks with the uplifting melodies of Euphoric Hardstyle.

name: Tribal Guarachero
description: Features percussion from Cumbia and Mexican Folk Music with upbeat Electro House-derived production and fast, triplet-oriented rhythmic patterns.

name: Uplifting Trance
description: Euphoric melodies, buildup-breakdown-anthem structures, common usage of supersaws, and a BPM range of usually around 135-142.

name: Progressive Psytrance
description: Emerged in the early 2000s, featuring a slow tempo range and clean production.

name: Jumpstyle
description: Features a heavy 909 kick drum, four-on-the-floor beat, and a tempo between 140 and 150 BPM.

name: G-House
description: Deep, dark form of House music that primarily incorporates rap samples or original rapping on top of minimal, bassy beats.

name: Jungle
description: Developed in the early 1990s from Breakbeat Hardcore, featuring rapid, chaotic-sounding breakbeats and strong basslines; known as the forerunner of most Drum and Bass styles.

name: Dubstyle
description: Characterized by the incorporation of the half-time rhythms and wobbly basslines of Dubstep.

name: Microhouse
description: Stripped-down, minimal form of House, originating in the late 90s, which takes influence from Minimal Techno, Glitch and IDM.

name: Nightcore
description: Based around the technique of speeding and pitching up pre-existing songs.

name: Electro
description: Syncopated rhythms programmed on the Roland TR-808 drum machine, often resembling Funk breaks, along with simple funky basslines.

name: Indietronica
description: Leftfield or DIY approach characterised by simpler Pop-informed melodies, often incorporating vocals or live instrumentation alongside Electronic production.

name: Ritmada
description: Short, repetitive and resonant beats with more danceable, immersive grooves drawn out of Afro-Brazilian instrumentation, a cleaner, more radio-friendly sound and greater focus on lyrics.

name: Dub Techno
description: Fuses elements of Minimal Techno with characteristics of Dub.

name: Trancestep
description: Combination of Trance melodies and structure with the breakbeats, fast tempos, and rolling basslines of Drum and Bass.

name: Hands Up
description: Prominent, melodic supersaw leads, Dance-Pop derived hooks, and a strong reliance on sidechained basslines and hard, powerful drums.

name: Tech House
description: Emerged in the mid-1990s taking influences from Techno, broadly characterized by minimalistic and repetitive melodies, focus on the bassline, and constant groove recognizable by prominent off-beat hi-hats.

name: Vaporwave
description: Sample-based, involves heavily synthesized and processed manipulation of corporate mood and background (elevator) music; though source material can also include genres such as Pop, Contemporary R&B, and Synth Funk.

name: Bubbling
description: Originating in the Netherlands in the late 1980s, fusing sped-up Dancehall with Dutch Antillean music and contemporary Dutch rave.

name: Acid Techno
description: Incorporates "squelching" synths from Acid House into a mechanical and usually more energetic Techno context.

name: Freeform Hardcore
description: Emerged in the mid-1990s, combining fast and hard-hitting rhythms with heavy influence from the synthesizers of Trance.

name: Italo House
description: Originated in Italy during the late 1980s, making use of anthemic pianos, diva vocals, and Dance-Pop song structures.

name: Twerk
description: Fast-paced; draws influence from Bounce and Miami Bass.

name: Future Garage
description: Combines the rhythms of late 1990s and early 2000s 2-Step with novel moods and production approaches, commonly featuring jittery, broken rhythms, vocal chops, and warm synth pads and plucks.

name: Forest Psytrance
description: Mimicks the ambience of a forest by using swarming and teeming effects as well as natural samples; features straightforward song structures.

name: Freestyle
description: Electro-influenced; developed in Latino communities in New York and Miami in the 1980s, especially dominated by Latin Freestyle and its Latin influences.

name: Florida Breaks
description: Developed during the mid-1990s, combining major influences of Hip Hop derivatives such as Miami Bass and Electro along with elements of 1990s Electronic Dance Music genres like House or Techno.

name: Hardbag
description: Development of handbag / Diva House characterized by heavier, hard hitting percussion, more pronounced rave and early Hard Dance elements, and fewer vocals.

name: Melodic Techno
description: Trance-influenced subgenre that was established in the mid-to-late 2010s, characterized by gradually progressing structure, and hypnotizing, atmospheric sound with arpeggiated, minor melodies, playing at around 120-125 BPM.

name: Latin Electronic
description: Combines Electronic with Latin American influences; started at the end of the 1990s.

name: Breakbeat Hardcore
description: Originated from the combination of sampled breakbeats and energetic rhythms with the "rave" influences set by early-1990s Techno and Acid House, including upbeat "stab" riffing and sample-based production.

name: Doomcore
description: Often somber and contemplative in mood, a feature achieved by the common use of long-stretched ambient pads, and sometimes even samples of screaming and other "spooky" sounds.

name: Lo-Fi House
description: Primarily distinguished from earlier styles of outsider house by its stricter reliance on deep house elements, cranky, nocturnal, and nostalgic atmospheres, and a distinct dreamy, lo-fi aesthetic.

name: Kuduro
description: Angolan music combining traditional Kilapanga and Semba with Techno and House.

name: Halftime
description: Offshoot of Drum and Bass (mainly Neurofunk) characterized by off-kilter rhythms playing at around 80-85 BPM (half of normal DnB pace), influenced by Dubstep, Wonky, Trap, and Hip Hop.

name: Beat bruxaria
description: São Paulo-originated Funk brasileiro with aggressive, distorted and high-pitched beats featuring minimalistic percussion and diversified sampling.

name: Hybrid Trap
description: Blending Trap's typical percussion and sub-basses with major influences from heavy bass music genres, and in particular, metallic, gritty, and off-kilter sound design, originally derived from Brostep.

name: Raggatek
description: Influenced by Reggae, Ragga and Dancehall, combining half-time instrumental sections with Hardtek's fast four-on-the-floor rhythms, as well as vocal samples, clean basslines and skank rhythms.

name: Industrial Techno
description: Harder, darker style of Techno which draws parallels with factories and the rhythm of machinery.

name: Electro latino
description: Mixes elements of Merengue and Reggaetón with polished production from mainstream House subgenres; often shares traits with Latin Pop, having anthemic melodies and verse-chorus structures.

name: Darkside
description: Dark atmosphere and fast rhythms; served as a forerunner of Drum and Bass.

name: Funkot
description: Fast-paced Indonesian form of Electronic Dance Music that combines Eurodance and Trance synths with the syncopated rhythm from Dangdut koplo.

name: Ibiza Trance
description: Builds on the relaxing island atmosphere of Balearic Beat, though with a slightly faster tempo of around 130 BPM.

name: Darkstep
description: Hard, uptempo and uses heavy breakbeats, Post-Industrial-influenced sound design, and horror movie samples to create a sinister, claustrophobic atmosphere.

name: Barber Beats
description: Borrows elements from Downtempo, Trip Hop, and Instrumental Hip Hop; emulates 1990s and 2000s Chillout music.

name: Complextro
description: Features densely-layered sound design, fast-paced cut-up sections, aggressive stabs, and common crossover with Brostep and Fidget House.

name: Euro House
description: Vocal-focused style emerging in the late 1980s in Europe, developing in parallel with Eurodance.

name: UK Hardcore
description: Four-on-the-floor beat around 175 BPM, thick, prominent basslines, and uplifting synth leads, with a cleaner and more accessible sound than other forms of Hardcore.

name: Eurodance
description: Takes elements of Euro-Disco, House, Techno, and Hi-NRG while featuring on-beat kick, hi-hat and snare sounds at around 110 to 150 bpm.

name: Euphoric Hardstyle
description: Melodic, more lighthearted, and accessible Hardstyle.

name: Horror Synth
description: Combines eerie and haunting synth pads and sharp, abrasive sounds to create atmospheres that accentuated the dark, unnerving, violent, or ghostly themes of horror films.

name: Terrorcore
description: Utilizes rhythms from earlier Gabber with a faster BPM (usually no more than 300) and darker approach.

name: Electro House
description: Warm analog basslines, punchy percussion, crisp high-pitched synthesizer leads, and clipped vocal samples.

name: Hardgroove Techno
description: Features shuffling grooves with heavy emphasis on layered percussive elements; often includes a mix of samples and drum machines played at a range of 135-140 BPM.

name: Bubblegum Dance
description: Playful, childlike style with a Bubblegum-like atmosphere that often incorporates high-pitched, light-hearted, and usually female-fronted vocals.

name: Dutch House
description: High-pitched wonky synth melodies and heavy influence from Fidget House which developed in the Netherlands around 2007-08.

name: EBM
description: Melodic and dancefloor-friendly take on Post-Industrial music that developed in the early 1980s.

name: Electro-Disco
description: Disco instrumentation is largely - if not entirely - replaced by synthesizers and augmented with futuristic elements such as the vocoder.

name: Chiptune
description: Used primarily in the 1980s within the context of 8-bit Video Game Music, combining simple synthesized waveforms and noise in a compositionally limited but overtly melodic style.

name: Ambient House
description: Primarily British genre developed in the 1990s with beats, synthesizers, and vocals in styles similar to House, treated with effects that are often described as "dream-like" or "chilled out".

name: Rawstyle
description: Incorporates earlier Hardstyle and Hardcore influences into the sound of 2010s hardstyle, creating a distinctly harsh, simplistic, raw, and dark sound, with fast BPM.

name: Goa Trance
description: Forerunner of Psytrance styles that emerged in the early 1990s, distinguished by old-school, organic sound with rapid, oriental-inspired melodies.

name: Tribal House
description: Prominent, syncopated rhythms of tribal drums such as toms and bongos on top of a House beat, usually resulting in a percussive, energetic sound, while ranging in mood.

name: Philly Club
description: Builds on Baltimore Club with a faster tempo and sometimes influences from more modern genres like Trap.

name: Psybient
description: Heavy influence from Psytrance and Goa Trance incorporated in an atmospheric Ambient context.

name: Minimal Wave
description: Retrospective term for more Electronic and minimal forms of New Wave, Post-Punk, Synthpop, and related styles, tending towards a more sparse and lo-fi sound.

name: Bleep Techno
description: Established in the late 1980s in the breakdancing and early rave scene of Yorkshire in the North of England, notable for its "bleepy" synth lines and heavy sub-bass.

name: Ragga Jungle
description: Developed in the early 1990s in London, combining Jungle breakbeats with heavy influence from Ragga.

name: Hardstep
description: Very compressed and sparse drumming, extensive use of kick drums and gritty, minimalistic production style with emphasis on simple, deep, modulated bass patterns.

name: Tropical House
description: Offshoot of Deep House featuring slow tempos around 100-115 BPM, light percussion and acoustic instrumentation, setting out to create a relaxed, summery, beach-like atmosphere.

name: Electropop
description: Dense, layered, and compressed production, usually coupled with a distinct fuzzy and "warm" low-frequency synthesizer style.

name: Eletrofunk
description: Blends Funk brasileiro vocals with production from popular late 2000s and early 2010s House.

name: Festival Progressive House
description: Uplifting, melodic, energetic but clean drops, featuring modern supersaws and pianos, taking influences from Progressive House, Uplifting Trance, but applying them to a form structurally closer to Big Room House and Electro House.

name: Hardcore
description: Umbrella term for a wide variety of fast-paced and energetic Electronic Dance Music styles that have been developed since the early 1990s.

name: Makina
description: Spanish Techno developed in the early 1990s; strongly influenced by New Beat and EBM, but aiming for a lighter sound.

name: Melbourne Bounce
description: Offbeat basslines giving it a "bouncy" groove, and minimalistic, side-chained melodies often making use of Dutch House-influenced synths, horns or vocal chops, resulting in a flippant, party-oriented atmosphere.

name: Speed Garage
description: Primarily British style that paved the way for UK Garage, combining cut-up vocal samples and sped up four-on-the-floor Garage House rhythms with Jungle-esque basslines.

name: Nu Skool Breaks
description: Developed in the late 1990s and early 2000s; takes elements from Drum and Bass, UK Garage, and Techno.

name: Dreampunk
description: Accentuates ambiance and a dreamy, futuristic atmosphere, taking influence from Progressive Electronic, as well as Cyberpunk and Dystopian movies.

name: IDM
description: Emerged through the development of Electronic Dance Music idioms into a less club-oriented and more experimental direction, often incorporating unconventional sound design and complex rhythms.

name: Breakcore
description: Mixes Hardcore with cut-up, complex, and frequently noisy breakbeats at very high tempos inspired by Jungle and Drum and Bass, often with heavy use of sampling.

name: Batida
description: Initially developed in the late '80s in Luanda, Angola, as a mixture of Soca- and Zouk-based percussion samples.

name: Birmingham Sound
description: Cold, mechanical, and hypnotic tracks, typically lacking the harsher and noisier elements of later Industrial Techno.

name: Ghetto House
description: Rough sound based around minimal drum machine-driven tracks and heavy usage of vocal samples.

name: Progressive Electronic
description: Synthesizer-driven genre focusing on developing, often longform compositions and drawing inspiration from various sources such as Progressive Rock, Western Classical Music, and Ambient.

name: Trap
description: Trap-influenced syncopated percussion with 808 samples and fast-paced hi-hats.

name: Rominimal
description: Hypnotic, minimal, and "dubby" loops, featuring subtle and intricate synth pads, cranky, clicky percussion, and bouncy, pulsing basslines.

name: Bit Music
description: Created by sound chips, cards, and sequencers found in video game consoles, personal computers, and arcade machines, as well as synthesizers emulating these sounds.

name: Dream Trance
description: Slow tempo (about 130 BPM) with strong roots in Progressive House, using beats typical of Eurodance and Dance-Pop; emphasizes a laid-back, carefree, and dreamlike sound.

name: Hi-Tech Psytrance
description: Glitchy sound design, morphing synth lines, strange sounds typically evoking futuristic or alien-like atmospheres, and faster tempos than most other forms of Psytrance.

name: Wave
description: Melancholic, atmospheric, melodic beats, defined by its Trap and Cloud Rap influence, ethereal and cold production style, and abundant use of sub bass.

name: Midtempo Bass
description: Harsh synth leads and a usual tempo of around 90-110 BPM.

name: Festival Trap
description: Massive, sub-bass-heavy drops with simple melodies, synth leads and production influenced by Big Room House; highly popular from 2013 to 2015.

name: Stutter House
description: Emerged in the 2020s taking influences from Melodic House, Trance, and Future Garage; features distinctive chopping of vocal samples and pads.

name: Dark Electro
description: Emerged in the early 1990s in Europe, incorporating the abrasiveness of old school Industrial but with a greater focus on a horror-inspired atmosphere and harsh, distorted vocals.

name: Drumfunk
description: Focuses on complex drum beats made to emulate live recordings.

name: UK Funky
description: Influenced by UK Garage-related styles and the regional styles of British immigrants, including Soca and West African-inspired Tribal House.

name: Techno
description: Highly beat-driven, repetitive, and mechanical, typically featuring minimal chord variation and melodies, focuses on rhythms and timbral variety; generally produced for club environments.

name: Wonky Techno
description: Strays from the standard beat of Techno, opting for a more fractured sound.

name: Breakbeat
description: Syncopated drum patterns (breakbeats), generally driving the groove at 110-160 BPM, emerging in the late 1980s from sampling drum breaks, before forming an umbrella genre separate from Drum and Bass, Downtempo, and Hip Hop.

name: Deep House
description: Emerged in the United States during the mid-1980s, featuring a relaxed tempo and influences from Jazz, Funk, and Soul.

name: Baltimore Club
description: Drew on the "booty bass" syncopation of Miami Bass, combined with Breakbeat rhythms around 130 BPM and chopped up vocal samples as found in Ghetto House.

name: Outsider House
description: Mostly derived from Deep House; intentional use of rough, lo-fi production, often utilizing a mix of analog/analog-like and digital instrumentation.

name: Melodic Bass
description: Outgrowth of Melodic Dubstep with euphoric sound design taking influences from Future Bass, guitar melodies and pop-driven structures

name: Acid Breaks
description: Incorporates prominent "squelching", resonant low pass filter sounds typical to Acid House, and often other "trippy" effects into intense, syncopated, broken rhythms in the tempos of 110-150 BPM.

name: Future Bounce
description: Fusion of Melbourne Bounce and Future House that combines the former's basslines and bouncy, upbeat drops with the latter's metallic, pronounced synth leads.

name: Bouncy Techno
description: Average tempo of 160 to 180 BPM, four-on-the-floor beats, melodic and repetitive synth melodies, drums with hard kicks, short breaks, progressive stomping rhythms, and offbeat notes.

name: Skweee
description: Mid-2000s offshoot of the Nordic Electro scene which uses simple, analogue or Chiptune-inspired synthesizer leads and basslines to create stripped-down, midtempo grooves drawing on Synth Funk and Hip Hop.

name: Acidcore
description: Developed out of the 1990s Acid Techno scene utilizing its prominence of "acid" TB-303 bassline sequences into more intense rhythms derived from earlier Hardcore, Gabber and Trance music.

name: Future Funk
description: Sample-based form of House which formed out of Vaporwave in the early 2010s.

name: Bass House
description: Busy, punchy percussion, prominent basslines and dirty, distorted synths resembling other bass music genres, with influences including Brostep, Fidget House, Bassline, Speed Garage, and Tech House.

name: Grime
description: UK Garage-derived production style characterized by dark, fast-paced, often aggressive beats that commonly features fast-paced MCing.

name: Shangaan Electro
description: South African genre with very fast BPM (around 180) featuring MIDI keyboard sounds and marimba.

name: Funktronica
description: Originated in eastern North America around the turn of the 21st century, combining trends of Synth Funk and Italo-Disco.

name: Industrial Hardcore
description: Fuses the sonic palettes and aesthetics of Industrial with the intense rhythms of Gabber, Speedcore, Doomcore and other subsequent styles.

name: Nu Style Gabber
description: Emerged near the end of the 1990s; deeper, more harmonic bassdrums with constant use of supersaw melodies, breakdown sections and slower tempos closer to the 150-180 BPM range.

name: Latin Freestyle
description: Displays a heavy influence of Hispanic American Music alongside the Disco, Hip Hop, and Electro influences of Freestyle.

name: Psybreaks
description: Uses Psytrance-derived sound design, such as squelchy sounds and psychedelic effects.

name: Brostep
description: Offshoot of Dubstep which replaces its sub-bass-oriented sound with an emphasis on catchy or aggressive mid-range melodies.

name: Mega funk
description: Blends funk vocal performances with production derived from mainstage 2010s House genres like Brazilian Bass and Slap House.

name: Garage House
description: Disco-influenced style of House that developed in the 1980s, incorporating elements of Gospel, Soul, and Dub, before evolving into a more upbeat style that prominently features crisp syncopated hi-hats and synth stabs.

name: NRG
description: Derived from UK Hard House; featuring darker, more anthemic Trance beats and a tempo of 155-165 BPM.

name: Funky House
description: Funky, warm, catchy basslines on top of looped Funk or Disco samples, resulting in an upbeat, bouncy, and pacy groove.

name: Hard Trance
description: Rave-oriented form of Trance infusing its atmospheres with faster, thumping rhythms and strong melodic / bassline sections, initially spawning out of the first wave in early-1990s Western Europe.

name: Deep Tech
description: Incorporates UK Garage rhythms and synths stabs, influences from British rave music, UK Bass and UK Deep House, resulting in an urban, subdued, but danceable sound with deep, muted basslines.

name: Deep Drum and Bass
description: Sub-bass-rich, repetitive, minimalist, and rhythm-focused production.

name: Italo-Disco
description: Produced primarily in Italy and is characterized by the use of synthesizers, drum machines, and sometimes vocoders to create a mechanical, Electronic version of Disco with futuristic atmosphere.

name: French Electro
description: Mixes Electro House with major elements of genres such as Electroclash, Nu-Disco, Synth Funk, and even Electro-Industrial, resulting in a harsh, punchy, and funky midtempo sound.

name: Funk automotivo
description: Catchy, repetitive synths accompanied by a strong four-on-the-floor kick and loud, reverberated vocals.

name: UK Garage
description: Shuffling rhythms and influences from sources including Contemporary R&B and Hip Hop, as well as many forms of Electronic.

name: Witch House
description: Nocturnal, occult-themed/horror-inspired, and murky beats with altered, slowed down and heavily processed samples laid on top of slow Hip Hop-derived drum rhythms and droning, dense synthesizers.

name: Chillout
description: Broad category of many relaxed styles of Electronic music which predominantly draw influence from Electronic Dance Music subgenres and Ambient.

name: New Beat
description: Emerged in the mid-1980s in Belgium; characterized by dark basslines, hard-hitting grooves and a slow tempo.

name: Nu Jazz
description: Electronic styles which feature aesthetics and occasionally instrumentation inspired by Jazz.

name: Raggacore
description: Prominently incorporates samples from Ragga and Dancehall, typically through shouted vocal lines and Reggae-influenced instrumentals.

name: Hard Drum
description: Highly percussive, syncopated rhythms, influences from global contemporary genres, and lack of melodic elements, with a tempo lingering around 130 BPM.

name: Coupé-décalé
description: Bass-heavy and minimal style of Ivorian popular dance music, drawing heavily from Zouglou and Congolese rhythms.

name: Powerstomp
description: Prominent, "stomping", and slightly distorted kick, relentless, repetitive reverse basses on the offbeats (borrowed from Hardstyle) that drive the tracks, giving the genre its distinct energetic sound, and cacophonous, noisy riffs and synth leads.

name: Hard Beat
description: Short-lived, harder, harsh form of New Beat with Industrial, Acid House and EBM-influenced arrangement.

name: Chillstep
description: Relaxed Dubstep influenced by Downtempo and Ambient.

name: French House
description: Originated in France in the mid-1990s, characterized by the use of filtered (low-pass, phaser, etc.) samples of Funk and Disco.

name: Detroit Techno
description: Features a signature mechanical, detached sound that echoed the atmosphere of Detroit's nightlife and incorporated influences from Electro and Chicago House as well as Synthpop and Electro-Disco.

name: Fidget House
description: Makes heavy use of Breakbeat Hardcore-esque synth stabs, pitch bending of basslines, and cut-up vocal samples, as well as glitchy, or "choppy", 4/4 rhythms.

name: Minimal Techno
description: Stripped-down and sparse, often featuring a dark sound and a slow, steady development through the track.

name: Dariacore
description: SoundCloud-based scene developed in the early 2020s, combining prominent vocal samples and distorted, dense production with influences from a wide range of Electronic Dance Music genres, often incorporating musical and visual references to popular media and internet culture.

name: Vocal Trance
description: Melodic, polished, Pop-like, and vocal-focused with around 120-145 BPM and frequent overlap with Progressive Trance, Uplifting Trance, and Euro Trance.

name: Jump-Up
description: Emerged in the mid-1990s, characterized by loud "wobbling" basslines, simpler drum loops, and a light-hearted and warm sound.

name: Chillsynth
description: Mixes Synthwave with major Chillwave elements, characterized by a dreamy and relaxed sound.

name: Funky Breaks
description: Merging popular 1990s breaks styles like Big Beat with major Funk and Soul elements.

name: Slap House
description: Taking influences from Brazilian Bass and Future House, characterized by prominent, "bouncy" bassline and simple, upbeat melodies.

name: Neurohop
description: Features the complex bass of Neurofunk and a frantic and mechanical sound design.

name: Big Beat
description: Popular in Britain during the late 1990s, driven by edited breakbeats and prominent basslines.

name: Future Core
description: Blends the speed, basslines, and rhythms of UK Hardcore (mostly coming from the J-core sphere) with euphoric, woozy synths, 7th chords and arpeggios, and vocal chops of Future Bass, particularly Kawaii Future Bass.

name: Speed House
description: Mixes busy, dirty sound design and intense drums derived from Bass House with fast tempos from 140-160 BPM, pumping basslines, and other UK Hard House influences.

name: Future Rave
description: Emerged in the late 2010s by combining the mainstage EDM approach with influences from then-popular forms of Techno and old-school Trance/rave-inspired grooves and atmospheres.

name: Synth Punk
description: Appropriates the harsh elements of Punk Rock but replaces the predominance of guitars with synthesizers and drum machines.

name: Jungle Terror
description: Emerging mostly from Dutch House and Big Room House, incorporates Tribal House-influenced percussion, hard kicks, and animal sounds resulting in a wild, jungle-resembling atmosphere in a festival EDM-oriented context.

name: Footwork Jungle
description: Combining Footwork's archetypal percussion, skittering drum fills, handclaps, and syncopated rhythms, with Drum and Bass drum programming and breakbeats, merging the two into unconventional, frantic drumming and dense, off-kilter rhythms.

name: Minimal Synth
description: Minimal and distinctly DIY take on Synthpop with a bleak and naive aesthetic.

name: Footwork
description: Extensive use of drum fills, tom drums, handclaps, and snares within fast-paced, highly syncopated beats not bound to a 4/4 kick rhythm.

name: Heaven Trap
description: Blends Trap’s signature percussion and sub-basses with euphoric, uplifting, melodious drops prominently derived from Festival Progressive House, contrary to the gritty, "dirty" nature of other EDM trap styles.

name: Acid Trance
description: Incorporates "squelching" acid basslines into Trance's evolving structure.

name: Dark Psytrance
description: Uses more complex sound design based on dark atmospheres and fast tempos.

name: Glitch Hop
description: Tempos around 100-110 BPM, swung, bouncy drum patterns, and a focus on upbeat, danceable drops and buildups.

name: Ambient Techno
description: Blends the mechanical, repetitive beats of Techno with atmospheres and textures inspired by Ambient music, resulting in slower tempos and more understated rhythms.

name: Schranz
description: Characterised by fast tempos, aggressive "whooshing" beat patterns, heavy compression and straightforward song structures.

name: Brazilian Bass
description: Originated in the 2010s in Brazil, taking influences from Deep House and Tech House, characterized by deep, punchy basslines making use of detuning and filtering effects.

name: Organic House
description: Subtle drums and grooves, slow tempos, and acoustic, traditional instrumentation, resulting in an organic, warm sound and spiritual atmosphere.

name: Liquid Drum and Bass
description: Atmospheric and melodic genre that developed in the early 2000s; utilizes polished and smooth production, simpler structures, and originally sampled Funk and Soul.

name: Crossbreed
description: Harsh and dense mixture of Darkstep with Hardcore developed in the late 2000s.

name: Neo-Grime
description: Mixes Wave with Grime and other forms of UK Electronic music.

name: UK Bass
description: Prominent bass/sub-basses, a deep, dark, airy, and mechanical sound derived from Dubstep, and eclectic production influenced by UK Funky, Juke/Footwork, Techno, and Breakbeat.

name: Chillwave
description: Emulates vintage aesthetics and popular musical styles of the 1980s/1990s altered by modern recording technology to create a hazy dreamlike atmosphere.

name: Livetronica
description: Incorporates Electronic instrumentation into live Rock jamming, drawing on a wide variety of Electronic Dance Music and Downtempo styles.

name: UK Hard House
description: Defined by offbeat bass patterns, compressed 909-style drums, buildup-drop structures, hoover sounds, and tempos between 120 and 155 BPM.

name: Electroclash
description: Draws from 1980s styles such as Electro, New Wave, and Electro-Disco combined with Techno, resulting in minimal, throbbing beats and basslines, buzzing synthesizer parts, vocoders and often monotone, half-spoken, half-sung vocals.

name: Latin House
description: Incorporates elements of Latin music (various Hispanic American Music and Brazilian Music genres), including their melodies, vocals, and instruments, as well as additional Latin percussion.

name: Slushwave
description: Utilizes a distinct form of sample manipulation to create a “slushy” sound, usually sequenced in soft Ambient-based soundscapes.

name: Digital Hardcore
description: High-tempo fusion of Hardcore Punk and Electronic, combining harsh vocals and electric guitars of the former with electronic instruments.

name: Colour Bass
description: Combines aggressive wobbling basses with the euphoric drops of Melodic Dubstep and Future Bass.

name: Afropiano
description: Blends the easy-going, poppy, and melodic Afrobeats sound and rhythms with major Amapiano elements, like lush, jazzy, and laid-back atmospheres often featuring light piano chords.

name: Techstep
description: Futuristic, dark, and gritty sound; quantized and compressed drum breaks, distorted bass lines, and a focus on a sci-fi atmosphere and sampling sources.

name: Nustyle
description: Developed out of Early Hardstyle from the mid-to-late 2000s, by incorporating distorted, crunchy, pitched kicks, faster tempo around 150 BPM, and detuned supersaw/square synth leads playing stompy, dramatic melodies.

name: Jazzstep
description: Combines the rhythm and speed of Drum and Bass with the melodies, composition, and instrumentation of Jazz.

name: Melodic House
description: Arpeggiated synth lines, focus on a chilled and laid-back atmosphere, and influence from genres such as Progressive House and Deep House.

name: Hard Dance
description: Umbrella term for EDM mainly developed out of Hard Trance and UK Hard House; usually characterized by fast tempos, hard kick drums and a "bouncy" syncopated groove.

name: Guaracha
description: Originated in Colombia, featuring "fluttering" trumpets, accordion, and tambora drums.

name: Wonky
description: Off-kilter fusion of Dubstep and Hip Hop which first developed in the mid-to-late 2000s.

name: Hardwave
description: Harder, festival-oriented offshoot of Wave, combining its "reese" basses and icy synths with Trap drum patterns, build-up-drop structures, and fuller sound influenced by Trance and Hardstyle.

name: Early Hardstyle
description: Forerunner subgenre that emerged around the turn of the 2000s from Hard Trance and Gabber, distinguished by the signature reverse bass groove, slower tempo around 140 BPM and straightforward, repetitive sound.

name: Progressive Trance
description: Combination of Trance and Progressive House which emerged in Western Europe in the early to mid-1990s.

name: Eurobeat
description: Fast-paced with roots in Hi-NRG and Italo-Disco; became mainly popular in Japan in the 1990s.

name: Trance
description: Evolving, tension-building structures with rapid, minor arpeggios on top of a hypnotic, four-on-the-floor drum patterns at fast tempo around 130-150 BPM.

name: Dungeon Synth
description: Focuses on evoking epic and atmospheric soundscapes primarily associated with fantasy and medieval settings through the usage of synthesizers, keyboards, lo-fi production, and drum machines.

name: Drum and Bass
description: Fast, syncopated breakbeat patterns (often sampled or programmed) with prominent basslines, commonly within the 160-180 BPM range.

name: Progressive Breaks
description: Derivative of Progressive House that incorporates its lush, melodic synth leads and atmospheres into Breakbeat's syncopated and broken rhythms.

name: Darksynth
description: Incorporates atmospheric Horror Synth and faster tempos into a much heavier and darker sound.

name: Jersey Sound
description: Soulful, melodic, Gospel-infused House music developed in Newark, NJ in the late 1980s and early 1990s with an emphasis on songcraft.

name: Darkcore
description: Aggressive, dark, and often midtempo with influences from Doomcore, Industrial Hardcore, and Nu Style Gabber.

name: Hardbass
description: Bouncy hard bass beats, fast tempo, usually 150-175 BPM, and occasional lyrics or rapping.

name: Dancefloor Drum and Bass
description: Emerged in the late 2000s as a more accessible and polished style with melodic, prominent synth leads derived from Jump-Up and punchy, often synthesized drums.

name: Hyper Techno
description: Exaggerated fast form of Belgian Techno for the Japanese market, focusing mainly on synth sounds and samples typical of 1990s dance music.

name: Illbient
description: Combines Ambient soundscapes with Hip Hop drum patterns, breaks, and samples through the busy effect layering of Dub.
